new_value: {"description":"Upload the completed notebook and scored dataset back to Kaggle. The output is a Kaggle notebook (derived from load_tmdb.ipynb) + a Kaggle dataset (tmdb-nrc-emotional-intensity-movie-keywords) containing ~930K movies scored with NRC Emotional Intensity per keyword. Use kagglehub or the Kaggle API to push both. This is the primary deliverable of the project."}

{"id":"imdb-pkc","title":"Fix NRC Emotion Intensity Lexicon download (HTTP 406)","description":"The notebook cell that downloads NRC-Emotion-Intensity-Lexicon.zip from saifmohammad.com returns HTTP 406 Not Acceptable. Need to either bundle the file in data/external/, add correct request headers, or find an alternative source. The NRC Word-Emotion Lexicon (binary) already works from the local file.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"1240090+bdelanghe@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:43:59Z","created_by":"Robert DeLanghe","updated_at":"2026-03-07T23:43:59Z"}

new_value: Fixed: added Accept: */* header alongside User-Agent; downloaded and verified NRC-Emotion-Intensity-Lexicon-v1.txt (9,829 rows) into data/. data/ is gitignored.

old_value: {"id":"imdb-6gq","title":"Rename notebook and improve pipeline naming","description":"User requested better names: rename load_tmdb.ipynb to something more descriptive (e.g. tmdb-nrc-emotion-intensity-pipeline.ipynb) and improve internal cell/section naming to reflect the actual pipeline purpose: TMDB keyword extraction + NRC Emotional Intensity scoring.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"1240090+bdelanghe@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:52:08Z","created_by":"Robert DeLanghe","updated_at":"2026-03-07T23:52:08Z"}

new_value: Renamed load_tmdb.ipynb to tmdb_emotion_intensity_pipeline.ipynb; updated notebook title cell and CLAUDE.md references.

old_value: {"id":"imdb-563","title":"Handle phrase-level TMDB keywords (tokenize + lemmatize + fallback)","description":"TMDB keywords are often phrases ('coming of age', 'post apocalypse', 'serial killer') but NRC lexicon is word-level. Direct lookup misses most keywords. Fix: tokenize each keyword into words, lemmatize tokens, look up each token in NRC intensity lexicon, average token scores per keyword, fall back to zero if no tokens match. This determines coverage quality of the entire pipeline.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"1240090+bdelanghe@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:55:29Z","created_by":"Robert DeLanghe","updated_at":"2026-03-07T23:55:29Z"}

new_value: Added phrase-level keyword scoring to notebook: tokenize, multi-POS lemmatize (n/v/a), lookup in NRC intensity dict, average matched tokens, zero fallback. Added nltk to requirements.txt.

old_value: {"id":"imdb-c1d","title":"Add NRC VAD (Valence-Arousal-Dominance) lexicon scoring","description":"Integrate NRC-VAD-Lexicon.zip from saifmohammad.com alongside the intensity lexicon. VAD adds 3 continuous dimensions per word: Valence (positivity 0-1), Arousal (energy/activation 0-1), Dominance (control/power 0-1). Combined with 8 emotion intensity scores, each movie gets an 11-dimensional affective embedding. Same phrase-tokenization strategy as imdb-563 applies. Output columns: vad_valence, vad_arousal, vad_dominance.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"1240090+bdelanghe@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:59:35Z","created_by":"Robert DeLanghe","updated_at":"2026-03-07T23:59:35Z"}

new_value: Added VAD lexicon loading (load-vad-lexicon) and phrase-level keyword scoring (score-vad-keywords) reusing tokenize/lemmatize helpers. Returns NaN for unmatched keywords.

old_value: {"id":"imdb-bt9","title":"Build keyword→emotion intensity scoring pipeline","description":"Core logic: join TMDB movie keywords against NRC Emotion Intensity Lexicon (real-valued 0-1 scores for 8 emotions). Aggregate per-movie: mean/max intensity per emotion across all keywords. Output columns: movie_id, title, keywords, anger, fear, joy, sadness, disgust, surprise, anticipation, trust.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"1240090+bdelanghe@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:43:59Z","created_by":"Robert DeLanghe","updated_at":"2026-03-07T23:43:59Z"}
